stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of Llama and Facebook tags indicates relevance to major LLM development.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and LLM ecosystem (Llama, safetensors).

stories.title: Mira Murati’s deposition pulled back the curtain on Sam Altman’s ouster
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: The story details the circumstances surrounding Sam Altman's abrupt ouster from OpenAI in late 2023.
Mira Murati's deposition provided insights into the events leading to Altman's removal.
The core reason cited for the ouster was Altman's alleged lack of consistent candor with the board.

stories.title: SpaceX has a $55 billion plan to build AI chips in Texas
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: SpaceX is planning a massive $55 billion investment into its 'Terafab' chip manufacturing plant in Austin, Texas.
This initiative signals Elon Musk's significant push into the highly competitive AI chip manufacturing sector.
The plan is detailed in a public hearing notice filed in Grimes County, indicating a major industrial undertaking.

stories.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission to prioritize profits over benefiting humanity.
The high-stakes court battle involves Sam Altman and Elon Musk, potentially reshaping the future direction of OpenAI and ChatGPT.
The lawsuit centers on accusations that OpenAI has shifted its focus from altruistic AI development to commercial gain.

stories.title: Elon Musk tried to hire OpenAI founders to start AI unit inside Tesla
stories.author: George Hammond, Financial Times
bullet_points: Elon Musk attempted to recruit OpenAI founders to establish an internal AI division within Tesla.
Musk's proposal was contingent on gaining control over the new for-profit AI unit.
The story highlights the intense competition and strategic maneuvering among major tech players in the AI space.

stories.title: TSMC taps wind power as AI chip demand soars, Taiwan feels energy crunch
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: TSMC is integrating wind power into its operations to meet surging energy demands.
The energy crunch is directly linked to the massive global demand for AI-driven chip manufacturing.
This highlights a critical intersection between advanced semiconductor production and sustainable energy infrastructure in Taiwan.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tooling to the open-source community.

stories.title: Canvas is down as ShinyHunters threatens to leak schools’ data
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Canvas, the Instructure-owned LMS, is currently experiencing an outage due to a major data breach.
The breach exposed sensitive student data, including names, emails, ID numbers, and private messages.
The hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for the attack, noting it is a repeat breach of Instructure.

stories.title: Apple’s AirPods with cameras for AI are apparently close to production
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple's rumored AirPods featuring cameras for AI integration are reportedly nearing mass production readiness.
Prototypes are currently in the design validation test stage, a precursor to production validation.
The cameras are not intended for traditional photography but are tied to AI functionalities.

stories.title: ChatGPT’s ‘Trusted Contact’ will alert loved ones of safety concerns
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: OpenAI is introducing an optional safety feature for ChatGPT.
Users can designate a 'Trusted Contact' to be alerted of severe safety concerns.
The feature is designed to notify contacts if the user discusses topics like self-harm or suicide with the AI.

stories.title: Canvas (Instructure) LMS Down in Ongoing Ransomware Attack
stories.author: stefanpie
bullet_points: Instructure's Canvas LMS is currently experiencing downtime.
The outage is attributed to an ongoing ransomware attack.
This highlights the vulnerability of major educational technology platforms to cyber threats.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code using open models within Ollama.
It bridges the gap between local, open-source LLM deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's working directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: DHS can’t create vast DNA database to track ICE critics, lawsuit says
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A lawsuit challenges the Department of Homeland Security's (DHS) ability to build a large-scale DNA database for tracking ICE critics.
The legal action specifically alleges that DHS is integrating a DNA database with ICE surveillance technology.
The story touches upon significant privacy and government surveillance concerns related to biometric data.

stories.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Mozilla has adopted AI-assisted bug discovery tools, specifically mentioning Mythos.
The tool reportedly found 271 vulnerabilities in Firefox with a very low false positive rate.
This signals a growing industry trend toward integrating AI into software security testing.

stories.title: SpaceX is starting to move on from the world's most successful rocket
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly shifting operational focus to Vandenberg Space Force Base.
Vandenberg is anticipated to become SpaceX's primary launch site for the near future.
This suggests a potential strategic evolution or diversification in SpaceX's launch infrastructure.

stories.title: Anthropic raises Claude Code usage limits, credits new deal with SpaceX
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic is increasing usage limits for its Claude Code capabilities.
The company has secured a new partnership with SpaceX.
This deal aligns with Anthropic's existing collaborations with major tech players like Microsoft and Amazon.

stories.title: Dirtyfrag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: flipped
bullet_points: The story concerns 'Dirtyfrag,' a vulnerability.
It is identified as a Universal Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) exploit.
The source is Hacker News, indicating a technical/security focus.

stories.title: Dirty Frag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: openwall.com via lattera
bullet_points: The article discusses a 'Dirty Frag,' which is a type of vulnerability.
The vulnerability is described as a Universal Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE).
This indicates a security flaw affecting various Linux systems.

stories.title: How to Authorize AI Agents Using Token Exchange Open Standards
stories.author: kimmaida
bullet_points: Focuses on securing AI agents by addressing over-permissioning issues.
Proposes using Token Exchange Open Standards for authorization.
Relevant for developers building complex, API-calling AI agents.
